$schema_note: Phase 2 — Announcements, Extended Student Profile, Digital ID (kiosk scan mode). module-registry.json only defines these at summary level, so this file is intentionally shallow (goal + rough backend/frontend/db task stubs, not full task-by-task detail). Expand each work package to the same depth as the Phase 1 files (02-08) when Phase 2 is actually scheduled — do that expansion as its own WBS task, not by guessing ahead of real requirements.

phase.name: Announcements, Extended Student Profile, Digital ID

work_packages.name: Announcements

stub_tasks.title: Expand into full WBS (DB schema, targeting rules, read-tracking model, CRUD API, composer UI, feed UI)
stub_tasks.notes: Do this expansion when Phase 2 is scheduled, using the same work-package/task shape as 02-08.

open_questions: Rich text or plain text composer?
Does read-tracking need per-recipient rows (scale: ~2,000 students × N posts) or an aggregate count?

work_packages.name: Student Profile (extended)
work_packages.goal: Extended profile beyond OSAS fields; documents; emergency contacts.

stub_tasks.title: Expand into full WBS (new local-only fields, document upload/storage, emergency contact CRUD, UI)

open_questions: Which documents specifically (ID copies, medical certs, COR)?
Who can view emergency contacts — same RBAC matrix as students, or a narrower set?

work_packages.name: Digital ID
work_packages.goal: Per-student QR identity card enabling kiosk scan mode (faculty scans students, reversing the current student-scans-QR flow).

stub_tasks.title: Expand into full WBS (per-student stable-but-rotatable QR identity, kiosk scanning UI/hardware assumptions, reconciliation with existing scan-based attendance)
stub_tasks.risk: Architecturally distinct from the current student-scans-faculty's-QR model — needs its own threat model, not a copy of qr_anti_fraud.

open_questions: Is a student's Digital ID QR static (like a badge) or still time-rotating?
What hardware is the 'kiosk' — a tablet, a dedicated scanner, faculty's own device?
Does this replace or run alongside the existing dynamic-QR-per-session model?
